native_task_output: gene/module smooths and pseudotime differential trends
same_data_ted_object_task: trajectory-associated pathway event with mode, onset, block gates and claim ceiling
native_task_interpretation: Strong native-task baseline for detecting trajectory dynamics; not penalized for lacking TED-specific claim fields.
TED_object_task_interpretation: On the same simulated trajectory task, adding TED event calibration and claim gates improves event-mode interpretation and artifact rejection while reducing overclaiming.
conclusion: Fair comparison: the native method detects dynamics; TED is more reliable for turning dynamics into auditable event objects.

native_task_output: pathway/module activity scores and smoothed score changes
same_data_ted_object_task: pathway-family event with event-FDR, family compression, mode, artifact gates and claim ceiling
native_task_interpretation: Strong native-task baseline for pathway scoring; it answers whether pathway activity changes.
TED_object_task_interpretation: On the same module-activity task, TED better separates redundant pathway signal from calibrated event mode and artifact-resistant claims.
conclusion: Pathway scoring is useful upstream, but direct scoring plus event calling is less reliable for TED-object claims.

native_task_output: rank-based cell-level activity calls for sparse marker programs
same_data_ted_object_task: dropout-aware sparse marker event with conservative claim ceiling
native_task_interpretation: Strong native-task baseline for assigning gene-set activity to cells under sparse detection.
TED_object_task_interpretation: On the same sparse-marker task, TED improves recovery and strongly reduces overclaiming by treating dropout and control behavior as evidence gates.
conclusion: Rank activity remains useful, but TED-object construction is needed for robust event claims under dropout.

native_task_output: state-matched perturbation contrast after composition adjustment
same_data_ted_object_task: matched-state perturbation event with mode, block robustness and allowed claim
native_task_interpretation: Strong native-task baseline for reducing composition artifacts and estimating perturbation effects.
TED_object_task_interpretation: On the same matched-state task, the baseline rejects artifacts well, but without mode and claim ceiling it overclaims more often.
conclusion: OT/matching is a strong upstream component; TED adds event interpretation and claim control.

same_data_ted_object_task: lineage priming event with sister/branch mode and priming claim ceiling
native_task_interpretation: Strong native-task baseline for fate association, especially when lineage/fate probabilities are the endpoint.
TED_object_task_interpretation: On the same rare-branch task, TED improves event recovery, mode assignment and artifact rejection by representing the branch result as a claim-aware event.
conclusion: Fate mapping is not replaced; TED formalizes when fate-associated dynamics justify a biological event claim.

native_task_output: ATAC/RNA lag or peak-gene association without full null-gated event object
same_data_ted_object_task: chromatin-to-RNA lag event with peak-gene null, concordance gates and claim ceiling
native_task_interpretation: Baseline can recover lag signal and may match TED on raw recovery.
TED_object_task_interpretation: On the same multiome task, TED improves artifact rejection and overclaim control by requiring peak-gene null and claim-ceiling gates.
conclusion: This is the clearest case where signal recovery alone is insufficient; TED prevents lag signal from becoming an unsupported chromatin-first claim.

native_task: time-course optimal transport or state coupling
same_data_ted_object_task: transport-informed dynamic event grammar with allowed biological claim
native_task_interpretation: Native task is trajectory coupling over time, not pathway-event claim calibration.
TED_object_task_interpretation: TED can consume time-coupled probabilities as upstream evidence, but claim ceilings still require event-FDR, controls and robustness.
conclusion: Included as positioning rather than metric attack: WOT solves a complementary upstream task.
